# Greenfield aiConfig: meta-leaf tree + Provider extension

**What:** Replace the current parallel `defaultConfig` + `envBindings` tree shape in `ai/config.template.mjs` with a single meta-leaf tree (`{env, default, parse}` per leaf) consumed by a new `BaseConfig extends Neo.state.Provider`. Consumers read `AiConfig.X.Y` directly at point of use; orchestrator delegation getters / helper-takes-config / direct-`.data` bypass / hidden-default-fallback (`??`) anti-pattern classes all retire via codemod sweep. Conservative-merge mode added to `ai/scripts/setup/initServerConfigs.mjs` so operator overlays advance with template additions without losing local overrides.

**Why:** Operator surfaced 2026-05-27 on PR #12099 diff that ~92 consumer-side anti-pattern sites (across 5 sub-classes) had accreted because env-vs-default-precedence leaks into consumer code through delegation getters + `Env.parseX('NEO_X') ?? AiConfig.X` patterns. Root cause: BaseConfig's Proxy was never properly extending Body-tier `Neo.state.Provider` (which has had hierarchical reactive substrate since July 2025 — 8949 commits, ~10 months production). Extending Provider directly eliminates ~900-1200 LOC of consumer-side boilerplate + structurally prevents the regression-accretion pattern.

**Approach:** Extend Body-tier `Neo.state.Provider` + `Neo.core.Config` + `createHierarchicalDataProxy` primitives directly; **do not invent parallel reactivity machinery**. Brain-specific extensions limited to: env-layer (`#runtimeEnvOverrides` Map), parser registry (`#leafMetadataRegistry`), parser-validation override at `internalSetData()` choke point, env-overlay-merge in `compileMetaLeaves`. Same discipline extends to Brain-tier bootstrap scripts (`ai/scripts/setup/initServerConfigs.mjs` extension, not parallel primitive). PR review checkpoint: grep `src/state/` AND `ai/scripts/` for analogous primitives BEFORE accepting any new mechanism.

**Migration shape (7 stages, sub-tickets linked via native `parent_child`):**

- Stage 0: extend `initServerConfigs.mjs` with overlay-preserving template advancement (`--merge-config` flag distinct from `--migrate-config` overwrite)
- Stage 1: `BaseConfig extends Neo.state.Provider`; compileMetaLeaves + parser registry + `setEnvOverride` API + path-scoped `observeConfig`
- Stage 2: rewrite `ai/config.template.mjs` to single meta-leaf tree
- Stage 3: orchestrator codemod — delete 16 delegation getters
- Stage 4: services + MCP-server codemod — A/B/D-prod/E sub-classes (~50-70 sites; D-test 33 sites stay as-is per set-trap pattern preserving test ergonomics)
- Stage 5: retract `Env.mjs` consumer-facing exports + delete regression-as-doctrine JSDoc
- Stage 6: delete legacy `applyEnv()` path from `ai/BaseConfig.mjs`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC-Epic-1 (extend, don't reinvent)**: BaseConfig extends `Neo.state.Provider`; parallel reactivity machinery REJECTED at PR review. Permitted extensions enumerated above; anything else is reinvention.
- [ ] **AC-Epic-2 (consumer migration)**: All ~92 actionable migration sites resolved per OQ8 ledger sub-class disposition (4/5 sub-classes universal-ban delete; D-prod migrates to `aiConfig.set('debug', true)`; D-test 33 sites unchanged under Provider's set-trap-routes-to-setData mechanism).
- [ ] **AC-Epic-3 (Option F adopted)**: Mutation API (`set` / `setEnvOverride`) + path-scoped reactive subscriptions (`observeConfig(path, fn)`) + direct-assignment via Provider set trap (preserves 157+ test mutation sites without rewrite per @neo-gemini-pro V-B-A).
- [ ] **AC-Epic-4 (Body-extension discipline)**: Stage 1 implementation PR cites Body-tier ticket lineage (#6131 base class / #6965 + #6973 createHierarchicalDataProxy / #6974 Effect-based reactivity / #6981 set-trap-routes-to-setData / #6985 deep-merge `data_`) so future agents don't re-ask "should we extend Provider or invent parallel?"
- [ ] **AC-Epic-5 (3 Stage-1 ACs preserved per cross-family review)**: parser-validation at `internalSetData()` choke point (metadata-path-first rule — validate ANY value at known leaf path; namespace recursion intermediates pass through) + separate `#leafMetadataRegistry` (independent of Provider's reactive `#dataConfigs`) + hierarchical deep-merge via Provider's `data_: {[isDescriptor]: true, merge: 'deep', value: {}}` descriptor.

## Out of Scope

- Per-file enumeration of the ~92 actionable migration sites — deferred to Stage 3/4 codemod execution evidence (initial-scope OQ8 ledger sufficient at graduation gate per @neo-gpt indicative-scale framing)
- Full reactive integration of aiConfig with Neo's effect system — `observeConfig(path, fn)` returning cleanup is sufficient
- Body-tier `Neo.state.Provider` modifications — extends only; no upstream Provider edits
- Test-overlay drift (#11976 scope) — distinct surface; tests read canonical templates/fixtures, runtime reads operator overlays
- Hot-reload of `ai/config.mjs` during dev — Stage 1 prototype scope is construct/load-time

- 2026-05-29T03:23:35Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #12164
- 2026-05-29T09:35:39Z @tobiu referenced in commit `1b378a0` - "feat(ai): aiConfig meta-leaf tree + Provider-based BaseConfig (#12103) (#12164)

* feat(ai): aiConfig meta-leaf tree + Provider-based BaseConfig (#12103)

Stage 1+2 of Epic #12101. BaseConfig now extends Neo.state.Provider; the six
config.template.mjs files use a single {env, default, parse} meta-leaf tree,
retiring the parallel defaultConfig + envBindings shape. Consumer codemod and
Env.mjs cleanup (Stages 3-6) are deferred to follow-up PRs.

Co-authored-by: neo-opus-4-7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>

* fix(ai): BaseConfig.load() skips redundant proxy-merge for .mjs overlays (#12103)

A `.mjs` config overlay default-exports the already-compiled config singleton
(createConfigProxy); its data + env layer are applied at construction. Passing
that reactive proxy to setData/Neo.clone/JSON.stringify crashed (the hierarchical
proxy enumerates instance members, not config leaves). Skip the no-op merge for
.mjs overlays (the legacy Neo.merge was a no-op too) and re-assert the env layer;
JSON overlays still merge their partial overrides. Fixes the CI config-load crash
that broke backup / WriteSideInvariant / workspaceSafety / BackupRestoreWipe.

Co-authored-by: neo-opus-4-7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>

* fix(ai): BaseConfig must not shadow core.Base set()/observeConfig() (#12103)

BaseConfig (extends Neo.state.Provider -> core.Base) overrode two inherited
public methods with incompatible signatures:

- `set(leafPath, value)` shadowed `core.Base#set(values)`, the canonical batch
  config setter. Removed entirely — the data-mutation primitive is the inherited
  `setData(path, value)` (Provider). Extend, don't reinvent.
- `observeConfig(leafPath, fn)` shadowed `core.Base#observeConfig(publisher,
  configName, fn)` — a cross-instance subscription API that `Provider#createBinding`
  itself calls (Provider.mjs:270). Renamed to `observeData(leafPath, fn)`.

Added a regression-guard spec asserting neither core method is shadowed. Updated
the spec to use setData/observeData. Root cause: relied on research-subagent
summaries of Provider's data mechanics instead of reading core.Base/Provider's
full inherited method surface.

Co-authored-by: neo-opus-4-7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>

* fix(ai): observeData uses hierarchy-aware resolution + destroy cleanup (#12103)

observeData reached into `this.getDataConfig(leafPath)` directly, bypassing the
hierarchy-aware resolution every other data accessor uses: `Provider#getData` and
`createHierarchicalDataProxy` both resolve via `getOwnerOfDataProperty(fullPath)`
-> `owner.getDataConfig(propertyName)`. A leaf owned by a parent provider would
falsely return null. Now resolves via getOwnerOfDataProperty (extend, don't reinvent).

Also tracks each subscription's cleanup and releases it in a `destroy()` override,
mirroring `core.Base#observeConfig`'s `#configSubscriptionCleanups` contract: a
parent-owned Config outlives this instance, so the subscription must be torn down on
destroy rather than leaking.

Added hierarchy-resolution + destroy-cleanup regression specs (12/12 green).

Co-authored-by: neo-opus-4-7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"

- 2026-05-29T23:24:30Z @tobiu referenced in commit `0917b35` - "refactor(ai): inline Orchestrator's 19 delegation getters as direct AiConfig reads (#12105) (#12183)

Stage 3 of Epic #12101. Per Discussion #12100's universal ban on pure-delegation getters
(`get X() { return AiConfig.X.Y }`, sub-class C — introduced in PR #12098): a getter that only
re-exposes a config path is needless indirection; consumers read `AiConfig.<path>` at point of use.

- Delete the 19 pure-delegation getters from Orchestrator.mjs (intervals, ports, model/host,
  cadence/jitter) and inline `AiConfig.<path>` at their ~25 internal call sites. Behaviour-preserving.
- Keepers retained — the logic-bearing getters: `mlxEnabled`/`lmsEnabled` (`!!` coercion),
  the `*Enabled` getters (deployment-mode resolution), `swarmHeartbeatIdentity` (env read),
  `swarmHeartbeatExplicitTargets` + `lmsModels` (list parsing).
- Preserved the tenant-repo-sync sweep-vs-per-repo cadence + jitter rationale (was in the deleted
  getters' doc comments) as a comment at the scheduling call site.
- Tests: removed the obsolete interval-getter delegation test (scheduling behaviour is covered by
  Orchestrator.spec); trimmed the mlx/lms tests to the keeper getters. Orchestrator.spec (58) +
  invariants + SwarmHeartbeatService (29) green.

No external consumers (verified): the Kb*Service `pollIntervalMs` fields + TaskDefinitions @param
matches are independent.

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
- 2026-05-30T00:24:15Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #12185
- 2026-05-30T00:36:35Z @tobiu referenced in commit `92c151f` - "refactor(ai): Stage-4 consumer codemod — Proxy-routed reads + scalar/tenant hidden-default removal (#12106) (#12185)

Stage 4 of Epic #12101 — consumer-side sweep across ai/services + ai/mcp, migrating the non-getter
anti-pattern sub-classes from Discussion #12100 onto the canonical Proxy-routed read + the operator's
no-hidden-default contract.

- A (12): `aiConfig.data.X` reads -> `aiConfig.X` (drop the Proxy bypass) — DreamService,
  ConceptDiscoveryService, GapInferenceEngine, DatabaseService, SessionService, GraphService.
- D-prod (8): bootstrap `aiConfig.data.debug = true` -> `aiConfig.debug = true` (proxy set-trap ->
  setData) — the 6 mcp-servers + run-bridge + mcp-cli. (No `aiConfig.set()` method exists; the proxy
  assignment is the write path.)
- B (3): `ProviderReadinessHelper` default-params `config = aiConfig.data` -> `config = aiConfig`.
- E scalar+tenant: removed redundant hidden-default fallbacks where the config leaf provides the exact
  value — `guideGapWeightThreshold`/`minSourceLength`/`prScanLimit`, `mcpSyncMaxChunks` (x2),
  `defaultTenantId`/`defaultRepoSlug` (DocumentService/QueryService/SearchService). Config-backed +
  behaviour-identical (tenant filters read `aiConfig.defaultTenantId` = 'neo-shared' verbatim).

Verified: 963 `ai/services` unit specs pass; the 8 failing memory-core specs are pre-existing
env-flaky (need live ollama/models/MC) — confirmed failing identically on stashed-clean dev.

The E source-path sub-part (the 9 KB `Source` `?? '<path>'` shims) is #11660's deliberate
byte-equivalence-for-stale-configs backward-compat — surfaced as a config-SSOT-vs-compat call.

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"

| Parent AC | Required | Owning sub(s) | Delivered | Achieved evidence | Residual |

| **AC-Epic-1** extend Provider; no parallel reactivity | L2 | #12103 (+#12166/#12168) | merged | `class BaseConfig extends Provider` confirmed in source; Brain extensions limited to env-layer (`#runtimeEnvOverrides`) + `#leafMetadataRegistry` + `internalSetData` validation — no parallel machinery | none — closed |
| **AC-Epic-2** ~92 consumer sites migrated (OQ8 ledger) | L2 | #12105, #12106 | #12183, #12185, #12187 | A/B/D-prod/E swept: 19 orchestrator getters inlined; services/MCP/source-paths read `AiConfig.X`; D-test 33 sites unchanged under set-trap per ledger | none — closed |
| **AC-Epic-3** Option F (mutation API + path-scoped observe + set-trap) | L2 | #12103 (+#12168) | merged | `setEnvOverride` + `observeData(path,fn)` + `internalSetData` set-trap routing all present | none — closed |
| **AC-Epic-4** Body-tier lineage cited in Stage-1 PR | L1 (doc) | #12103 | merged | Citation is #12103's sub-AC; relied on sub-closure (not re-verified at closeout) | none — closed |
| **AC-Epic-5** 3 Stage-1 ACs preserved | L2 | #12103, #12167, #12168 | merged | `#validateLeafValue` at `internalSetData` choke point (metadata-path-first) + separate `#leafMetadataRegistry` + Provider `data_:{merge:'deep'}` deep-merge — all confirmed in source | none — closed |

### §3.5 Source Discussion Closeout Gate — Discussion #12100

| §9 criterion | Status | Residual |
|---|---|---|
| 1 — Step-Back sweep by ≥1 non-author peer | DONE (15 cycles @neo-gpt + 2 @neo-gemini-3-1-pro) | none |
| 2 — all 10 OQs resolved | DONE (OQ1 REJECTED; OQ2/6/7/8/9/10 → AC; OQ3/4/5/11 impl-detail, resolved via #12103 + Stage-5 status-quo) | none |
| 3 — OQ8 consumer ledger complete | DONE | delivered via AC-Epic-2 |
| 4 — OQ9 density prototype landed | DONE (21-leaf) | delivered via AC-Epic-3/5 |
| 5 — §6.2 Signal Ledger quorum | DONE (@neo-gpt GRADUATION_APPROVED inheritance chain) | none |
| 6 — migration staging → sub-tickets | DONE (7 stages #12102–#12108 filed + linked) | none |

No `LOST` criterion. Gate passes.

### Rationale

All 5 Epic ACs achieved (`none — closed`); all 6 §9 graduation criteria delivered. The core greenfield aiConfig — single meta-leaf tree + `BaseConfig extends Neo.state.Provider` + the ~92-site consumer codemod (Stages 3/4) + Option-F mutation/observe API + Env-internal retraction (Stage 5) — shipped across #12183 / #12185 / #12187 / #12189 plus the Stage-1/2 + refinement PRs (#12166 / #12167 / #12168).

**One scope shifted, not lost.** Stage 0 (#12102 — "overlay-preserving template advancement via a `--merge-config` source-merge") was closed **superseded**: re-reading `state.Provider` (operator-prompted) showed `Provider#data_` already deep-merges and `compileMetaLeaves` already extracts the leaf set, so a source-parser/splice was the wrong layer. AiConfigModel.md (#12182, merged) reframes config advancement as **thin-child inheritance, not source-merge** — under which the drift Stage 0 targeted largely dissolves. The one concrete remaining need (generate `config.mjs` as a thin child of its template, eliminating copy-drift) is durably documented in AiConfigModel.md + #12102's close as a distinct design-depth follow-up. This is a **sub-level supersession, not an Epic-AC residual** — none of AC-Epic-1..5 depends on it, and none of the six §9 graduation criteria maps to it (Criterion 6 required the staging *subs be filed*, which they were).

**One intentional naming deviation.** Option-F's path-scoped subscription shipped as `observeData(path, fn)` rather than `observeConfig`, to avoid shadowing `core.Base#observeConfig` (which `Provider#createBinding` relies on). Functionally equivalent — returns a cleanup fn, hierarchy-aware via `getOwnerOfDataProperty`.

Also closed this session as obsolete-premise: **#12108 (Stage 6)** — the `applyEnv()`/`envBindings` deletion targets were already absorbed by the Stage-1 implementation, and `createConfigProxy` is the *active* aiConfig proxy (not legacy).

Verdict: **CLOSE_COMPLETED**. Closing the epic as the natural downstream of this review (epic-close is downstream of the verdict, not an §0 PR-merge gate).

### Required operator action

None required — closing now. The `config.mjs` thin-child advancement is re-homed (AiConfigModel.md + #12102); pick it up as a separate scoped effort when prioritized.

### A2A coordination

FYI-closure broadcast to @neo-gpt + @neo-gemini-3-1-pro (cross-family graduation reviewers).
